popularStory.headline: My Maternal 18th. Great Scottish Grandfather, Sir Colin Iongantach "Colin the wonderful" Campbell, 13th. Knight of Lochawe
popularStory.description:
Name: Sir Colin Iongantach "Colin the wonderful" Campbell, 13th. Knight of Lochawe
Birth: January 1344 in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland
Married: 1365 in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland to 1st. spouse, Mariota M …

associatedMedia.headline: Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Mariota Margaret Campbell於1345年1月出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛格易爾海亞德時，她的父親John Campbell15歲，母親Mariota Campbell17歲。她與Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe在蘇格蘭培爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1420年在蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍享年75歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾蒙吉爾蒙 帕里什 邱奇 安德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mariota Margaret Campbell于1345年1月出生在苏格兰阿吉爾洛格易爾海亞德时，她的父亲John Campbell15岁，母亲Mariota Campbell17岁。她与Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe在苏格兰培爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1420年在苏格兰阿吉爾洛霍享年75岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾蒙吉爾蒙 帕里什 邱奇 安德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Mariota
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Mariota Margaret Campbell was born in January 1345, in Lochgoilhead, Argyll, Scotland, her father, John Campbell, was 15 and her mother, Mariota Campbell, was 17. She married Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe in Perthshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1420, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, at the age of 75, and was buried in Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mariota Margaret Campbell est née en janvier 1345, à Lochgoilhead, Argyll, Écosse, son père, John Campbell, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Mariota Campbell, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe à Perthshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1420, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mariota Margaret Campbell im Januar 1345 in Lochgoilhead, Argyll, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Campbell 15 und ihre Mutter Mariota Campbell 17. Sie hat in Perthshire, Scotland Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1420 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland und wurde in Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Mariota Margaret Campbellが1345年1月にスコットランドアルグイルロチゴイルヘドで生まれた時，父John Campbellは15歳，母Mariota Campbellは17歳であった。スコットランドペルスシュアイアでSir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochaweと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1420年にスコットランドアルグイルロチャウで75歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトキルムンキルムン パーイッシュ チャーチ アンド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mariota Margaret Campbell(이)가 1345년 1월 스컷랜드 아그이 럭고일헤드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Campbell(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mariota Campbell(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 스컷랜드 퍼스실르에서 Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1420년 스컷랜드 아그이 럭아w에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬먼 킬먼 파리시 c후르치 앤드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mariota Margaret Campbell nasceu em janeiro de 1345, em Lochgoilhead, Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, John Campbell, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Mariota Campbell, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe em Perthshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1420, em Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Kilmun Parish Church and Cemetery, Kilmu: килмун пэриш чурч анд кеметири, килмун, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Ardkinglass, Lochgoilhead, Argyllshire,: ардкинглас хоус, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mariota Margaret Campbell родилась январь 1345 г. в местности лочгоилхид, аргилл, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John Campbell было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Mariota Campbell, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe в местности пиртшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1420 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности килмун пэриш чурч анд кеметири, килмун, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mariota Margaret Campbell nació en enero de 1345, en Lochgoilhead, Argyll, Scotland, su papá, John Campbell, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Mariota Campbell, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir Colin Campbell 13th. Knight of Lochawe en Perthshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1420, en Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultada en Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido.
